zh.Walden, Essex, England: 英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登
zh.Willingale Doe, Essex, England: 英國艾可維林加爾 多
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Hoo於1435年出生在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登時，他的父親Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger"19歲，母親Dorothy Norwood17歲。他與Maude Bardolf結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1516年5月20日享年81歲，埋葬在英國波克夏貝頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Hoo于1435年出生在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登时，他的父亲Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger"19岁，母亲Dorothy Norwood17岁。他与Maude Bardolf结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1516年5月20日享年81岁，埋葬在英国波克夏貝頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hoo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Wai Lam, Wing, Beng, Chin, Gan, Hing, Hui, Kee, Lai, Lai Ching, Mei-Mei.
variant Romanization of the surname   Hu  .

variants.name: Hu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Ming, Ping, Bin, Yi, Ying, Jing, Xiao, Qi, Yan, Chi.  Korean Chang, Chung, Min, Yiping, Chong, Chul, Hu, Jung Soo, Sang Hoon, Tian, Wook, Yiming.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 胡, a term referring to the ethnic minorities in ancient northern and western China: (i) from Hu (胡), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Henan province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng (located in present-day Shaanxi and Henan provinces) in the 26th year of the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720 BC ), people from the fief adopted 胡, the name of the state, as their surname. (ii) from Hu (胡), the name of a state (located in present-day Anhui province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu in the 15th year of the reign of Duke Ding of Lu (ruler of the state of Lu, reigned 509–495 BC ), people from the fief adopted 胡, the name of the state, as their surname. (iii) from the personal name of Hu Gong Man (胡公满, Duke Hu of Chen), the posthumous title of Yu Man. He was given the fief of Chen (located mainly in present-day Henan and Anhui province) by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (iv) from the personal name of Hu Guang (胡廣), who changed his original surname 黃 to 胡 during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ). (v) from the Hu Gu (胡骨) or He Gu (纥骨) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their surname to 胡 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 扈, meaning ‘retinue’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Hu (扈), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Shaanxi province) dating back to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ) (ii) adopted as a surname by the Hu Di Gan (扈地干) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hoo was born in 1435, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, his father, Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger", was 19 and his mother, Dorothy Norwood, was 17. He married Maude Bardolf. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 20 May 1516, at the age of 81, and was buried in Beedon, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Hoo est né en 1435, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger", avait 19 ans et sa mère, Dorothy Norwood, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Maude Bardolf. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 20 mai 1516, à l’âge de 81 ans, et a été enterré à Beedon, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Hoo 1435 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger" 19 und seine Mutter Dorothy Norwood 17. Er hat Maude Bardolf geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 20. Mai 1516 im Alter von 81 Jahren und wurde in Beedon, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hoo è nato il nel 1435 nel Saffron Walden, Essex, England, il suo padre, Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger", aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Dorothy Norwood, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Maude Bardolf. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 20 maggio 1516, all'età di 81 anni, e fu sepolto a Beedon, Berkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Hooが1435年にイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger"は19歳，母Dorothy Norwoodは17歳であった。Maude Bardolfと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1516年5月20日に81歳で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアビードンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hoo(이)가 1435년 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger"(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Dorothy Norwood(은)는 17세였다. 그는 Maude Bardolf(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1516년 5월 20일 81세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 브이던에 묻혔다.

mn.Hoo, Kent, England: Hoo, Centlond, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hoo nasceu em 1435, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger", tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Dorothy Norwood, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Maude Bardolf. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 20 de maio de 1516, com 81 anos, e foi sepultado em Beedon, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Hoo родился в 1435 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger" было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Dorothy Norwood, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Maude Bardolf. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 20 мая 1516 г. в возрасте 81 год и был похоронен в местности бидон, биркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Hoo nació en 1435, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Hoo "The Younger", tenía 19 años y su mamá, Dorothy Norwood, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Maude Bardolf. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 20 de mayo de 1516, a la edad de 81 años, y fue sepultado en Beedon, Berkshire, Inglaterra.
